headline: 2 S&P 500 Stocks for Long-Term Investors and 1 We Question
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) is home to the biggest and most well-known companies in the market, making it a go-to index for investors seeking stability. But not all large-cap stocks are created equal - some are struggling with slowing growth, declining margins, or increased competition.

headline: PepsiCo’s 3.9% Yield Is a Safe-Haven Masterclass
summary: The 10-year Treasury at 4.48% has made every dividend payer fight for capital. PepsiCo (NASDAQ: PEP), the global snack and beverage giant behind Pepsi, Lay’s, Gatorade, and Doritos, just announced its 54th consecutive annual dividend increase. With the stock at $144.27 and a yield near 3.96%, the question for income investors is simple: is this ... PepsiCo’s 3.9% Yield Is a Safe-Haven Masterclass

headline: Struggling Pizza Hut restaurant chain will be sold for $2.7 billion
summary: The struggling Pizza Hut restaurant chain will be sold for $2.7 billion by parent company Yum Brands.  Yum Brands said in February that it was considering selling Pizza Hut and the chain looked to close 250 U.S. restaurants.  Pizza Hut was founded in 1958 in Wichita, Kansas.

headline: Wall Street Lunch: Yum! Brands Serves Up Pizza Hut In $2.7B Deal
summary: Yum! Brands (YUM) is selling Pizza Hut for $2.7B, shedding a once-dominant pizza chain that has steadily lost market share to rivals.

headline: Yum! Brands Is Selling One Of America's Most Iconic Pizza Chains For $2.3 Billion— Here's What To Know
